items.description: Large, bold 'X' or cross with elongated descending tail curving left; executed in dark ink with a single stroke flourish.
items.scholarly_note: Typical medieval nota or attention mark used to flag a passage; not obviously a cancellation.

items.description: Irregular brownish liquid stain overlapping several words in the middle of the page.
items.scholarly_note: Does not appear to have caused significant ink loss or text obscuring.

items.region: lower left and central area, lines 22–28
items.description: Diffuse dark smudges and spotting, consistent with dirt, handling, or offset ink.
items.scholarly_note: Common on heavily used lower margins of medieval codices.

items.region: bottom edge and lower right corner
items.description: Substantial parchment loss, fraying, and irregular tearing along the entire bottom margin; final line of text is abraded and partially lost at the right.
items.scholarly_note: Classic edge wear from handling and former binding; no modern repairs visible.

items.region: lower third of page, across text and margin
items.description: Multiple faint horizontal abrasions and surface creases; some fibers raised where ink is lighter.
items.scholarly_note: Likely from rubbing or page turning rather than deliberate erasure.

items.description: Faint dark 'K' and possible additional letters or strokes visible below the main text block.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly a quire signature or accidental offset; difficult to read with certainty.

items.description: Small localized thinning or hole in parchment immediately adjacent to the marginal cross.
items.scholarly_note: May be natural parchment flaw or result of later handling.

scratches: multiple faint horizontal abrasions in lower third
localized surface scrape near left margin 'X'
general fiber disturbance along bottom edge
damage_notes: heavy loss and ragged tearing of entire bottom margin with partial text loss on last line
edge fraying on right and bottom
surface thinning and small hole near left margin
overall discoloration and handling wear consistent with unbound or frequently consulted leaf
summary: Prominent marginal cross mark on left; scattered liquid and handling stains; no clear doodles or pen trials; one possible faint quire signature at foot; significant physical damage confined mainly to the lower edge with parchment loss and abrasion.
